introSection: Montaña Rusa was a steel roller coaster previously located at Parque del Café in Montenegro, Quindio, Colombia. It opened at its current location in 1999. The ride was previously located at Worlds of Fun in Kansas City, Missouri, USA where it operated from 26 May 1973 to 1997 under the name Zambezi Zinger.

sections.title: History
sections.content: Demolition of the ride began in September 2025.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Yellow track and blue supports. The ride opened with green track and supports at Worlds of Fun, being repainted to feature red track and blue supports when relocated to Parque del Café, where it later got repainted to its current colours.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: When it first opened in 1977, the ride had four trains. It is not known how many are still in use.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content:
On 29 May 1973, two women received minor injuries when the power failed and two cars collided.

In May 1997, passengers were slightly injured with two trains collided.

On 19 December 2021, a train carrying 9-16 riders had a mechanical failure that caused the middle car of the train to derail, leaving 3 injured.

fullText:
Montaña Rusa was a steel roller coaster previously located at Parque del Café in Montenegro, Quindio, Colombia. It opened at its current location in 1999. The ride was previously located at Worlds of Fun in Kansas City, Missouri, USA where it operated from 26 May 1973 to 1997 under the name Zambezi Zinger.

Demolition of the ride began in September 2025.



Yellow track and blue supports. The ride opened with green track and supports at Worlds of Fun, being repainted to feature red track and blue supports when relocated to Parque del Café, where it later got repainted to its current colours.

When it first opened in 1977, the ride had four trains. It is not known how many are still in use.

On 29 May 1973, two women received minor injuries when the power failed and two cars collided.

In May 1997, passengers were slightly injured with two trains collided.

On 19 December 2021, a train carrying 9-16 riders had a mechanical failure that caused the middle car of the train to derail, leaving 3 injured.
